fields.datasource_indicator: Antenatal Care coverage - at least four times by any provider
fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least four times by any provider
fields.measurement_denominator: Total number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least four times by any provider

fields.datasource_indicator: Antenatal care coverage - at least once by skilled health personnel
fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended antenatal care during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least once by skilled health personnel
fields.measurement_denominator: Total number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended antenatal care during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least once by skilled health personnel. Skilled health personnel include Medical doctor and Nurse/Midwife.

fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended by skilled health personnel during their most recent live birth
fields.measurement_denominator: Total number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended by skilled health personnel during their most recent live birth

fields.datasource_indicator: Births attended by skilled health staff (% of total)
fields.methodology_estimation: Weighted Average obtained from UNICEF, State of the World's Children, Childinfo, and Demographic and Health Surveys.
fields.indicator_definition: Births attended by skilled health staff are the percentage of deliveries attended by personnel trained to give the necessary supervision, care, and advice to women during pregnancy, labor, and the postpartum period; to conduct deliveries on their own; and to care for newborns.

fields.methodology_estimation: Weighted Average. Data obtained from UNICEF, State of the World's Children, Childinfo, and Demographic and Health Surveys.
fields.indicator_definition: Pregnant women receiving prenatal care are the percentage of women attended at least once during pregnancy by skilled health personnel for reasons related to pregnancy.

fields.datasource_indicator: Pregnant women receiving prenatal care of at least four visits (% of pregnant women)
fields.methodology_estimation: Weighted Average. The data is obtained from UNICEF, State of the World's Children, Childinfo, and Demographic and Health Surveys.
fields.indicator_definition: Pregnant women receiving prenatal care, at least four times, are the percentage of women attended at least four times during pregnancy by skilled health personnel for reasons related to pregnancy.

fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women who have attended their 4th antenatal Visit
fields.indicator_definition: The percentage of pregnant women who attended their 4th antenatal visit.

fields.measurement_numerator: Antenatal 1st (booking) visit 20 weeks or later+Antenatal 1st (booking) visit before 20 weeks
fields.indicator_definition: The percentage of pregnant women who had their first antenatal visit
The percentage of live births in the health facility attended to by a skilled birth attendant

fields.datasource_indicator: Skilled Attendance at Delivery or Birth
fields.methodology_estimation: Using statistical estimation techniques, the estimates are derived from a variety of data sources, including vital registration statistics, surveys, disease registries, surveys, scientific literature etc. The data obtained from these sources are cleaned, then thoroughly reviewed with collaborators that are familiar with the contexts in which these data are collected. Estimates may differ from national statistics due to differences in data sources and methodology.
fields.indicator_definition: The percentage of deliveries attended by personnel trained to give the necessary supervision, care, and advice to women during pregnancy, and labor.

fields.datasource_indicator: Assistance during delivery from a skilled provider
fields.measurement_numerator: Number of live births in the five years preceding the survey assisted by a skilled provider.
fields.measurement_denominator: Live births in the five years preceding the survey
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of live births in the five years preceding the survey assisted by a skilled provider. Skilled provider includes doctor, nurse, midwife and auxiliary nurse or midwife.

fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women who had a live birth in the five years preceding the survey who had 4+ antenatal care visits
fields.measurement_denominator: Women who had a live birth in the five years preceding the survey
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of women who had a live birth in the five years preceding the survey who had 4+ antenatal care visits

fields.datasource_indicator: Antenatal care from a skilled provider
fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women with a live birth in the five years preceding the survey who received antenatal care from a skilled provider
fields.measurement_denominator: Women who had a live birth in the five years preceding the survey
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of live births in the five years preceding the survey who received antenatal care from a skilled provider

fields.measurement_numerator: Number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least once by a skilled health personnel
fields.measurement_denominator: Total number of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years
fields.indicator_definition: Percentage of women age 15-49 years with a live birth in the last 2 years who were attended during their last pregnancy that led to a live birth at least once by a skilled health personnel
